text: **Anna:** It's time for {part|部分/パート|/pɑːrt/|This is part two of the story.} two of the children's show.
**Announcer:** It's time, it's time.
**Anna:** I can't {wait|待つ/楽しみにする|/weɪt/|I can't wait to see you.}
**Anna:** Shh!
**Anna:** I can't {hear|聞こえる/聞く|/hɪər/|Can you hear me?} it.
**Announcer:** Last time on the Time Traveling Treehouse
**Announcer:** Anna was {lost|迷った/行方不明|/lɔːst/|I got lost in the city.}
**Announcer:** Really lost.
**Announcer:** She wants to learn about {baseball|野球|/ˈbeɪsbɔːl/|Baseball is a popular sport.}, but her computer Mendy sent her to many wrong places.
**Anna:** Mindy, help me.
**Anna:** I am in the {ocean|海|/ˈoʊʃən/|The ocean is very deep.} with a {shark|サメ|/ʃɑːrk/|Sharks live in the ocean.}
**MINDY:** I will try one more time, Anna.
**Anna:** Please try {harder|もっと一生懸命に|/ˈhɑːrdər/|Try harder next time.}, Mindy
**Anna:** Now, where am I?
**Anna:** I'm at a {playground|遊び場|/ˈpleɪgraʊnd/|Children play at the playground.} with a baseball field.
**Anna:** Yes!
**Anna:** I can {teach|教える|/tiːtʃ/|I teach English to students.} children about baseball here.
**Announcer:** Mindy!
**Anna:** It took you a long time!
**Anna:** But you did it!
**MINDY:** Thanks, Anna.
**MINDY:** Give me a call when you find the answer.
**Anna:** Okay
**Anna:** Come with me.
**Anna:** Let's learn how to play baseball.
**Anna:** This is a baseball field.
**Anna:** This is a {pitcher's|投手の|/ˈpɪtʃərz/|The pitcher's mound is important.} mound.
**Anna:** This is first base.
**Anna:** This is second base.
**Anna:** This is third base.
**Anna:** This is {home plate|本塁打のホームベース|/hoʊm pleɪt/|The runner touches home plate to score.}
**Anna:** To play baseball, you really only need a {bat|バット|/bæt/|He swings the bat hard.}, a {ball|ボール|/bɔːl/|Throw the ball to me.}, and a {glove|グローブ/軍手|/glʌv/|Catch the ball with your glove.}
**Anna:** Each team has many players.
**Anna:** {Pitchers|投手|/ˈpɪtʃərz/|Pitchers throw the ball to batters.} pitch the ball.
**Anna:** {Catchers|捕手|/ˈkætʃərz/|Catchers wear protective gear.} catch the ball.
**Anna:** {Batters|打者|/ˈbætərz/|Batters try to hit the ball.} bat the ball.
**Anna:** {Runners|走者|/ˈrʌnərz/|Runners move quickly around bases.} run the bases.
**Anna:** {Fielders|野手|/ˈfiːldərz/|Fielders catch flying balls.} field the ball.
**Anna:** First, the {pitcher|投手|/ˈpɪtʃər/|The pitcher prepares to throw.} pitches the ball to the batter
**Anna:** The batter bats the ball.
**Anna:** Then the runner runs to first base.
**Anna:** Each time a batter bats, the runners run around the bases.
**Anna:** Each time a runner runs across home plate, they {score|得点する|/skɔːr/|Teams score points in games.} a run.
**Anna:** There are nine {innings|イニング|/ˈɪnɪŋz/|Baseball games have nine innings.} in a game.
**Anna:** The team with the most runs at the end of nine innings wins the game.
**Anna:** Mindy, Mindy, we found the answer!
**Anna:** It's time to return to the {tree house|ツリーハウス|/triː haʊs/|Kids love playing in a tree house.}
**MINDY:** Great job.
**MINDY:** That was fast.
**Anna:** The end.
**MINDY:** Good job, Anna.
**Anna:** You know, I still don't like {children's shows|子供向け番組|/ˈtʃɪldrənz ʃoʊz/|Children's shows are educational.}, but I like this children's show
**Anna:** I liked the {time travel|タイムトラベル|/taɪm ˈtrævəl/|Time travel is a fun sci-fi concept.}
**Anna:** But time travel is not real.
**Anna:** You're so silly, Anna.
**Anna:** Yeah, time travel is so silly.
**Anna:** Thanks, goodbye.
**Anna:** Bye, thank you.
**Anna:** Hello, Mindy.
**Anna:** Are you there?
**MINDY:** Hello, Anna.
**Anna:** Mindy, I want to go to {China|中国|/ˈtʃaɪnə/|China is a big country.}
**Anna:** {Until|〜まで/〜するまで|/ˈʌntɪl/|I will wait until you come.} next time.
